channel.title: Tampa Bay News Monitor
channel.description: Press releases and breaking news, Tampa Bay

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] 'I'm not giving up': Florida mom battles Medicaid denials for special needs son
item.description: A Florida mother is appealing three Medicaid denials for a specialized safety bed prescribed as medically necessary for her 11-year-old adopted son, who has autism and developmental disabilities.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Air quality crisis deepens as wildfire smoke covers Great Lakes, East Coast
item.description: Smoke from Canadian and Minnesota wildfires blanketed parts of the Midwest and East Coast, triggering hazardous air alerts and health warnings for millions.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Sinkhole causes road closure on Bahia Vista St. in Sarasota: SSO
item.description: Sarasota County Sheriff's Office said eastbound lanes of Bahia Vista Street are closed due to a sinkhole.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Taco Bell lettuce supplier under investigation in cyclospora illness outbreak
item.description: Taco Bell removed lettuce from select locations after a multistate cyclospora outbreak linked to shredded iceberg lettuce sickened thousands.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Pinellas County preschoolers receive a big boost when it comes to literacy
item.description: The Early Learning Coalition of Pinellas County just received a $1.2 million grant to go toward improving child literacy.

item.title: [BREAKING] [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] 1 dead after shooting in Holiday, investigation underway: PSO
item.description: The Pasco County Sheriff's Office (PSO) said a man is dead after an overnight shooting in Holiday on Friday.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] News to Know for July 17
item.description: Recap of Trump's primetime speech. Plus, 4,000 residents without power in Pinellas after morning storms.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] 4,000 residents without power in Pinellas after storms
item.description: About 4,000 residents in Pinellas County were without power Friday after storms moved through the area, utility officials said.

item.title: [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Hillsborough tests voting machines, reminds voters of key Florida primary deadlines
item.description: As Florida's primary election draws closer, Hillsborough County election officials have completed one of the most important steps before voting begins: publicly testing the machines that will count ballots.

item.title: [BREAKING] [FOX 13 Tampa Bay] Holiday shooting: Man killed after argument, suspect detained: PSO
item.description: A man is dead after an early morning shooting in Holiday, and the suspected gunman is in custody, according to the Pasco Sheriff's Office.

item.title: [BREAKING] [ABC7 Suncoast (WWSB)] BREAKING: Watermain break creates sinkhole on Bahia Vista Street Friday morning.
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] NHC tracking disturbance off Florida. How it'll affect your weekend
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Is it legal in Florida to collect rainwater?
[Sarasota Herald-Tribune] Taco Bell pulls lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis. What about Florida?

[ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Two teens in stolen vehicle rescued after trying to swim across Manatee River: MCSO
item.description: Two teenagers driving a stolen vehicle were arrested on Wednesday after trying to escape by swimming across the Manatee River, authorities said.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] From wilderness settlement to riverfront city, Bradenton's history spans more than 180 years
item.description: From a wilderness settlement in 1842 to a thriving riverfront city, Bradenton's history is still visible in its streets, structures, and ballpark.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] The Nest at Robinson Preserve offers families trails, kayaking, and treetop views in Manatee County
item.description: Robinson Preserve's Nest Nature Center in Manatee County offers 500 annual programs, a shaded playground, and 20-plus miles of trails and kayaking routes.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] LECOM Ball Park in Bradenton builds community connections on and off the baseball field
item.description: LECOM Ball Park in Bradenton is expanding its community presence with 100-plus local events in 2025 and a new city park breaking ground in June.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Thousands of people cruise to Anna Maria Island on Bradenton’s Gulf Islands Ferry
item.description: The Gulf Islands Ferry connects Bradenton's Riverwalk to Anna Maria Island in 45 minutes. Tickets start at $11 for adults; kids under four ride free.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] Braden Castle ruins in Bradenton mark the site where a sugar plantation once stood
item.description: The ruins of Braden Castle in Bradenton tell the story of a sugar plantation, a Seminole attack, and a community that still thrives today.

item.title: [ABC Action News / Tampa Bay 28] GT Bray Recreation Center in Bradenton offers summer camps for kids, meals included
item.description: GT Bray Recreation Center in Bradenton offers summer camps for kids with free lunches and snacks through a school district partnership.

item.title: [Tampa Bay Beacon (Pinellas)] How should Pinellas cope with shrinking schools? Officials want input
